title: The Monster
author: Edgar Saltus
description: Edgar Saltus's "The Monster" delves into the psychological intricacies of obsession and obsession's transformative power, intertwining elements of Gothic literature with a distinctly modern sensibility. The novel presents a richly textured narrative that explores the titular monster not merely as a metaphor for physical deformity but as a reflection of society's moral decay and the grotesque nature of unbridled desire.
